heading: Overview
body:
In this guide, you can learn how to create an application that uses
**Mongoose** to implement MongoDB's **Queryable Encryption** feature.

Queryable Encryption allows you to automatically encrypt and decrypt document fields.
You can use Queryable Encryption to encrypt sensitive data in your application, store data fields
as randomized encrypted data on the server, and query the encrypted fields.
This tutorial shows you how to set up Queryable Encryption by using Mongoose, which provides
an Object Document Mapper (ODM) library for data interaction.
